title: ADR-001: Layer 7 HTTP Routing
decision: Implement Layer 7 HTTP routing with: (1) path and method-based matching, (2) middleware support, (3) context propagation, (4) error handling integration. Concretely, a Router struct holds routes []Route and middleware []Middleware, with Route carrying Method, Path, Handler, and Middleware fields. The Handle(method, path, handler) method appends to r.routes.
consequences: Routers can express HTTP traffic for API services with path-based dispatch
Middleware chains are first-class, enabling cross-cutting concerns (auth, logging) per route or globally
Context propagation is explicit, simplifying request-scoped values across handlers
Error handling integrates with the routing layer for uniform failure responses
Cleanly fits Go web frameworks via the HandlerFunc/Middleware shape
alternatives: Layer 3/4 (transport-level) routing — too low-level for HTTP service semantics such as path/method matching and middleware
Framework-default router (e.g., net/http ServeMux) — insufficient for the middleware chain, context propagation, and error-handling integration requirements
Third-party Go routers (e.g., gorilla/mux, chi) — viable but add external dependency; custom Router keeps stack lean and tailored
RPC/gRPC-based dispatch — rejected; HTTP traffic and path-based routing are explicit requirements
